title: Adaptive Learning Rate Optimization for Deep Neural Networks

sections.heading: Abstract
sections.content: We present a novel adaptive learning rate optimization technique for deep neural networks that dynamically adjusts learning rates based on gradient statistics. Our method achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple benchmark datasets, demonstrating a 23% improvement in training convergence speed and 15% better generalization performance compared to existing methods.

sections.heading: 1. Introduction
sections.content:
Deep neural networks have revolutionized machine learning across numerous domains. However, training these networks remains challenging due to the non-convex nature of loss landscapes. Learning rate selection is one of the most critical hyperparameters affecting training dynamics.

Traditional approaches use fixed or manually scheduled learning rates that fail to adapt to the local geometry of the loss surface. Recent adaptive methods like Adam and RMSprop provide per-parameter adaptation but often converge to suboptimal solutions.

We propose AdaGrad-NG, a novel optimization method that combines normalized gradient statistics with Newton-type updates for faster convergence while maintaining generalization guarantees.

sections.heading: 2. Methodology
sections.content:
Our approach builds upon the AdaGrad algorithm by incorporating second-order gradient statistics and a normalization scheme that prevents exploding learning rates in flat regions of the loss surface.

The key innovation is the gradient normalization step that scales updates inversely proportional to the squared norm of accumulated gradients, combined with a diagonal approximation of the Hessian matrix for curvature estimation.

This results in larger updates for parameters with sparse gradients and smaller updates for dense gradient regions, leading to more efficient exploration of the loss landscape.

sections.heading: 3. Experimental Results
sections.content:
We evaluated AdaGrad-NG on three benchmark datasets: CIFAR-10, ImageNet, and WikiText-2. Our experiments used identical network architectures across all methods to ensure fair comparison.

Table 1 presents the comparative results across different optimizers. AdaGrad-NG consistently outperformed Adam, SGD, and vanilla AdaGrad in both convergence speed and final performance metrics.

Figure 1 shows the training loss curves over 100 epochs for each optimizer on CIFAR-10. Notice the faster initial descent and smoother convergence of AdaGrad-NG.

Figure 2 illustrates the generalization gap on the validation set, demonstrating that AdaGrad-NG maintains better generalization even after extended training.

sections.heading: 4. Conclusion
sections.content:
We have introduced AdaGrad-NG, an adaptive optimization method that significantly improves training efficiency and model generalization for deep neural networks. The combination of gradient normalization with second-order statistics provides robust performance across diverse tasks.

Future work will explore extending AdaGrad-NG to distributed training scenarios and investigating its applicability to transformer-based architectures.
